"user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-50008", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:L/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-863", "name": "Incorrect Authorization"}], "description": "### Impact

The `routeAllowList` server option restricts external client access to a configured list of REST API routes. The check is only enforced as Express middleware against the outer HTTP request URL, so the `/batch` handler dispatches each sub-request to the internal router without re-running the allow-list check. An external caller whose outer route matches `batch` can issue batch sub-requests to any REST API route that the operator omitted from the allow-list.

Authentication, ACL, CLP, and other inner-route authorization controls still apply — only the operator-configured route firewall is bypassed.

### Patches

`routeAllowList` is now re-enforced for each batch sub-request inside the batch handler before dispatch, mirroring the existing per-sub-request rate-limit enforcement pattern. The path-normalization and regex-match logic was extracted into a shared helper used by both the outer middleware and the batch handler. Master and maintenance keys bypass the per-sub-request check on the same terms as the outer middleware.

### Workarounds

Operators who use `routeAllowList` and have allowlisted `batch` can mitigate without upgrading by explicitly including every inner route they intend to allow via batch in the allow-list (for example, `routeAllowList: ['batch', 'classes/Public.*', 'functions/allowedFunction']`). This approach makes those inner routes reachable as direct REST requests as well, so it is broader than the post-patch behavior, but it eliminates the bypass.

Operators who do not configure `routeAllowList` are not affected. Parse Server v8 LTS is not affected because `routeAllowList` was introduced in v9.8.0.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00342, "percentile": 0.25936}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-p84r-h6rx-f2xr", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-19T19:34:41Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-p84r-h6rx-f2xr", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-p84r-h6rx-f2xr"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-50008"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-12T19:16:29Z", "published_at": "2026-06-19T19:34:41Z", "references": ["https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/security/advisories/GHSA-p84r-h6rx-f2xr", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-50008", "https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/pull/10482", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-p84r-h6rx-f2xr"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/parse-community/parse-server/security-advisories/GHSA-p84r-h6rx-f2xr", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server", "summary": "parse-server: Server option routeAllowList is bypassable through batch sub-requests", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-19T19:34:42Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-p84r-h6rx-f2xr", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "9.9.1-alpha.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "parse-server"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 9.8.0, < 9.9.1-alpha.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: parse-server: Server option routeAllowList is bypassable through batch sub-requests
